title: Erlang/OTP for Controllers → a Go, Bead-Backed Reconciler
summary:
OTP's *concurrency* (a BEAM process per entity, unbounded mailboxes, the scheduler) does **not** transfer to a single-process, tick-driven reconciler — and cargo-culting it (a goroutine per session) reintroduces the exact races and mailbox-meltdown OTP warns about. What transfers is OTP's *design philosophy*, and it transfers almost perfectly once you notice one inversion.

**The inversion.** In OTP the *process is the state*: state lives in the process heap, dies with a crash, and the supervisor's job is to reinitialize it. In Gas City the *bead is the state*: it is durable, and the "process" (the per-tick reconcile of one entity) is ephemeral and stateless. So the two roles swap. This is precisely Candea & Fox's **crash-only software** (HotOS 2003): if all durable state lives in "state managers" (beads) and every component recovers by replay, then stop == crash and start == recover — there is no separate repair path. The convergence reconciler already embodies this: `Reconciler.ReconcileBeads` is a *normal* pass that also happens to be startup recovery.

**gen_statem is the core primitive, and it's already in-repo.** `internal/convergence/reconcile.go` is a textbook `gen_statem` in `state_functions` mode: a total switch over a typed durable state (`creating`/`terminated`/`waiting`/`active`) with `(action, error)` wrapped at one site, idempotency keys, and marker-last write ordering. The session reconciler's disease is the *absence* of this: a stringly-typed state where unknown ⇒ skip-forever is exactly gen_statem's "accidentally postpone an event forever" busy-loop. The fix is gen_statem discipline: an exhaustive state enum, a `default` arm that **escalates with a TTL** instead of silently skipping, and gen_statem's decisive State-vs-Data rule — *if a value changes which actions are legal it belongs in the typed State; otherwise it is Data* — which is exactly the design law the 29-raw-key metadata sprawl violates.

**The error kernel is the pure diff core.** Armstrong's / Akka's error-kernel pattern says: shrink the must-be-correct code to a minimum and push fallible work to disposable leaves. Gas City's error kernel is `deriveConvergeActions(durable, runtime) -> []action` (pure, total) plus a small idempotent executor plus the atomic bead-write path. Everything else — provider probing, template resolution, prompt rendering, tmux I/O — is a *leaf* that is *allowed to fail*, because failure just means "skip this entity, retry next tick." The 2,188-line god function is the anti-kernel: it fuses must-be-correct convergence with fallible I/O into one blast radius.

**"Let it crash" becomes "let the tick fail."** Because durable state survives, a panic mid-reconcile of entity E is harmless — *provided* you never half-repair and never take a destructive action on an uncertain observation. The swallowed-error bug (tmux blip → `providerAlive=false` → orphan-close of a live session) is a let-it-crash *violation*: it converts an observation failure into an irreversible action. Let-it-crash says the safe default under uncertainty is inaction + retry; destructive actions demand positive confirmation.

**Restart strategies mostly dissolve; restart *intensity* survives.** `one_for_one`/`rest_for_one`/`one_for_all` answer "when a child dies, whom else must I restart?" — a question that only exists because siblings hold in-heap state. Beads hold no in-heap state, so level-triggered re-derivation subsumes all four strategies (dependencies are bead/molecule DAG edges, recomputed every tick). The *one* piece that survives is `max_restarts`/`max_seconds`, and Gas City already has it: `crashTracker` quarantines an entity after N restarts in a window and is *correctly in-memory* (reset on controller restart, matching OTP intensity semantics). Quarantine is the supervisor "terminating and escalating" a poison entity that convergence alone cannot heal — a crash-loop.

corePatterns.name: gen_statem as a typed, bead-backed entity FSM (state_functions mode)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Each reconciled entity is a total state machine: an exhaustive typed state enum plus per-state transition logic. State governs which actions are legal; other fields are just Data.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Model session/molecule/scope like convergence/reconcile.go: a durable state enum lives IN the bead, a total switch derives actions. The default arm escalates with a TTL — never the current skip-forever on unknown state.

corePatterns.name: Error kernel — minimize the must-be-correct surface
corePatterns.whatItIs: Concentrate correctness-critical logic into a tiny kernel; push fallible I/O to disposable leaves whose failure is contained and retried, not defended against.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: The kernel is the pure total deriveConvergeActions(durable,runtime)->[]action plus an idempotent executor plus atomic bead writes. Probing, template/prompt rendering, tmux I/O are leaves; splitting the 2,188-line god function along this seam is the whole migration.

corePatterns.name: Let-it-crash / crash-only recovery
corePatterns.whatItIs: Recovery is the only path: crashing is a safe stop, restart re-derives from durable state. No defensive half-repair; abort and retry on failure.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: A failed tick leaves the bead in its last consistent durable state; the next tick re-observes. Requires total+idempotent reconcile and atomic writes. Crucially: never take a destructive action (close bead, kill session) on an uncertain observation.

corePatterns.name: Restart intensity (max_restarts / max_seconds) → quarantine
corePatterns.whatItIs: A supervisor gives up when a child crashes more than N times in a window, terminating and escalating rather than restarting into an infinite loop.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: crashTracker already implements this (maxRestarts within restartWindow), correctly in-memory so it resets on controller restart. Generalize it into a reusable intensity/backoff guard wrapping ANY reconciled entity, not just sessions — the one restart-strategy piece that survives level-triggering.

corePatterns.name: Single-writer owns state (serialized access, no races)
corePatterns.whatItIs: One process is the sole writer of an entity's state; all mutations funnel through it and are serialized, so data races are structurally impossible.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: The single controller tick is the sole writer per entity. Do NOT spawn a goroutine per session to imitate actors — that reintroduces the races and mailbox pathologies OTP fights. Keep the durable-vs-runtime reconcile in one Observe→Plan→Apply writer.

corePatterns.name: Bounded mailbox + backpressure
corePatterns.whatItIs: Unbounded actor mailboxes cause memory blowups and cascading failure under overload; robust systems bound intake and shed or defer excess work.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: The reconciler's mailbox is the ready-bead queue. Bound per-tick work with a fairness budget and defer overflow to the next tick, rather than fanning out every ready bead at once (the cache-reconcile wisp-flood incident is Gas City's own 300K-mailbox meltdown).

corePatterns.name: Write-ahead intent marker for exactly-once side effects
corePatterns.whatItIs: gen_statem's state-enter/inserted-event discipline plus a durable WAL: record intent before the effect, confirm after, so recovery neither loses nor duplicates it.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: The S19 priming case: stamp a durable priming_attempted intent BEFORE the nudge, confirm primed_at AFTER. Converts at-most-once (crash loses the prompt) or unbounded at-least-once into exactly-once. Make this a reusable primitive for every effectful action.

primitivesItSuggests: Entity FSM: a typed, bead-backed state machine = (durable StateEnum in the bead) + pure total Plan(observed)->[]Action + idempotent Apply, replacing per-arrival edge stamping with one level-triggered transition function (generalizing convergence/reconcile.go to sessions/molecules/scopes).
Observation as a typed sum — Known(x) | Unknown | ProbeError — so 'close a live session on a guess' becomes structurally unrepresentable; Plan may only emit destructive actions on Known.
Write-ahead intent marker: a reusable durable WAL field pair (attempted → confirmed) that turns any effectful action (nudge, spawn, close) into exactly-once across crashes.
Reusable restart-intensity / quarantine-backoff guard, generalized from crashTracker, wrapping any reconciled entity's Apply to stop poison crash-loops and escalate.
Bounded reconcile scheduler: a per-tick work budget with fairness/aging over the ready-bead queue — the single writer's bounded mailbox with backpressure.
Escalation / dead-letter primitive: unhandled or stuck states emit an event and carry a TTL instead of skipping forever, making 'silently stuck' observable and bounded.
Idempotency-key + marker-last write ordering as a shared library (already latent in convergence) so every materialize/close path is crash-safe by construction, not by review.
antipatterns: Spawning a goroutine (or literal actor) per session/molecule to 'be like the BEAM' — reintroduces in-heap state, data races, and unbounded-mailbox blowups that the single-writer tick loop exists to prevent.

Porting literal restart strategies (one_for_all / rest_for_one) when level-triggered re-derivation already recomputes every entity from durable state each tick — redundant machinery solving a problem that only exists for in-heap state.
Defensive programming / half-repair inside the reconcile body — the opposite of let-it-crash; each arrival path stamping its own subset of metadata is exactly how the 100+ gap-bugs are born.

Treating an unknown/unhandled state as skip-forever — gen_statem's 'accidentally postpone an event forever' busy loop; the default arm must escalate (TTL, dead-letter, event) instead of silently re-logging every tick.

Taking a destructive action (close bead, kill session) on an uncertain or errored observation — turns a transient probe failure into irreversible data loss; the safe default under uncertainty is inaction + retry.

Storing correctness-critical state in the controller's in-memory trackers — only crash-loss-tolerant, intensity-style counters (crashTracker) belong there; anything whose loss changes the outcome must be a bead.
Fanning out all ready work per tick with no budget or backpressure — recreates the mailbox-overload cascade; bound and defer instead.
Growing the error kernel 'just in case' — poorly-understood crash-only design becomes lazy shortcuts; the kernel must stay small enough to test exhaustively.
testabilityContribution: gen_statem's decisive win is that the transition is a PURE function of (state, event) — no processes, no clock, no I/O needed to test it. Gas City's deriveConvergeActions(durable, runtime)->[]action is exactly that, which unlocks a military-grade story: (1) TOTALITY — a table-driven test asserting every state-enum value has a non-skip arm, enforceable by an exhaustiveness lint so a new state cannot compile without handling; (2) IDEMPOTENCE — property test that Plan∘Apply∘Plan yields an empty action list (re-running after actions land is a no-op), the S19 mandate; (3) NO-DESTRUCTIVE-ON-UNCERTAIN — fuzz the observation with Unknown/ProbeError and assert Plan emits zero close/kill actions, killing the swallowed-error bug class by construction; (4) CRASH-ONLY RECOVERY — because there is no separate recovery path, you test recovery by injecting a panic/kill at any point and asserting the next tick converges (ReconcileBeads is already a normal pass); (5) ERROR-KERNEL FOCUS — exhaustive/near-formal testing is spent only on the small pure kernel, while fallible leaves need just one 'does failure degrade to retry?' test each; (6) DETERMINISTIC INTENSITY — the crashTracker/quarantine guard is time-injected and unit-tested (crash_tracker_test.go) with no real sleeps. Net: entire error classes (skip-forever, close-on-blip, at-most-once priming, half-stamped identity) become unrepresentable or property-checkable rather than review-proven.

title: Kubernetes controller-runtime → a Go, bead-backed single-controller reconciler for Gas City
summary:
Gas City already owns the two hard prerequisites for a controller-runtime architecture: a level-triggered tick and beads as the reconciled "objects." The S19 spec (`observe → diff → act`, pure total `deriveConvergeActions(durable, runtime)`, idempotence tables, legacy-parity pin, write-ahead `priming_attempted_at` marker) is, verbatim, a controller-runtime retrofit incrementally staged onto the god files. The K8s literature names four *missing pieces* that S19 either under-commits to or leaves out, and each kills a documented error class.

**(a) SSA field-ownership kills the "N paths stamp different metadata subsets" class — yes, decisively.** The disease is that fresh-spawn/adoption/resume/drift/rollback each edge-stamp their own subset of identity+priming keys, and bugs (#3872-1's missing alias) live in the gaps. SSA's rule — every field has exactly one manager, writes are *fully-specified intent*, unmentioned fields get dropped — maps to: one typed writer per coherent field-group (`SessionSpec`, `SessionStatus`, identity), taking a **whole typed struct** (not a `map[string]string`) and applied atomically. "Forgot the alias" becomes a compile error, not a silent tick-gap. Gas City is *one* process with N code paths, so it needs the discipline (single-writer-per-group, S19's C3/M6/M9) not the `managedFields` apparatus.

**(b) Finalizers kill the pane-leak-on-rollback class (#2073) — yes.** The leak is a K8s orphan: the owner (bead) was rolled back but the external resource (tmux pane) survived because teardown was edge-triggered on a path that didn't (or crashed before it) run. Finalizer semantics: the object is not *gone* until external cleanup provably ran. Transfer: model rollback as durable `absent` intent (deletionTimestamp analog) that **dominates all other actions** and blocks final closure until `actionRollbackRuntimeToAbsent` confirms teardown — level-triggered, retried every tick under the observed-gate. This also fixes the dual bug (a tmux blip → `providerAlive=false` → orphan-CLOSE of a live session): a finalizer inverts the dangerous default to "prove it's dead before you close." The trade-off ("cleanup delayed while controller down") is exactly Gas City's "work persists, convergence resumes" bargain.

**(c) A rate-limited requeue workqueue replaces the fixed 30s tick and fixes the respawn storm — the single biggest upgrade.** Keep the tick, but reinterpret it as the *informer resync* (drift-catch safety net, why dropped events are survivable). Add a per-session rate-limited queue: failing/waiting sessions get `AddRateLimited` (baseDelay·2^failures, capped, **jittered**); converged sessions `Forget`. Per-item jitter + a global token bucket de-synchronize the 152-sessions-died-together herd — killing the synchronized-respawn-storm and the retry-treadmill. `RequeueAfter` (transient wait, no backoff) vs `error` (backoff) is the right distinction. Bonus: reconcile becomes **per-key**, so the 2,188-line batch mega-function dissolves by construction. `k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue` is importable, or a small typed clone.

**(d) spec/status split — yes; it's the root of the dual-representation disease.** Today ~29 keys sit in one bag written by anyone (raw `beads.Bead` vs typed `session.Info`, 18 hand-proven predicate pairs). Split into `spec` (desired: identity, pool slot, prompt-configured, wake_mode — config-owned) and `status` (observed: started_config_hash, primed markers, live_hash, synced_at, circuit_state — reconciler-owned). This kills the spec-vs-status write race, gives the two typed reps a principled home, and makes reconcile literally `converge(spec, status)`. A version/`resourceVersion` CAS on status writes makes the *redundant-observers* invariant safe rather than hopeful, and a typed status enum with a **total switch** (convergence.go already does this) ends "unknown state → skip forever."

The redesign generalizes S19's proven shape across all field-groups and adds the queue, superseding the drifting reconcile copies (dispatch, caching_store, supervisor) by making them instances of one `Reconcile(key)` contract.

corePatterns.name: Level-triggered reconciliation (events are hints, not instructions)
corePatterns.whatItIs: React to current observed state vs desired, never to individual edges; a workqueue holds keys not events, so missed events, restarts, and partitions self-heal on the next pass.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Already the reliability story. Formalize it: every arrival path (spawn/adopt/resume/drift/rollback) becomes a hint that enqueues a session key; the tick's resync re-checks all keys so no path's omission is permanent.

corePatterns.name: Reconcile(req) → (Result{RequeueAfter}, error), per-object
corePatterns.whatItIs: A pure-ish function reconciling ONE object per call; return an error for backoff, RequeueAfter for polling waits, nil+Forget for done. Idempotence is mandatory.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Replaces reconcileSessionBeadsTracedWithNamedDemand (2,188 LOC/28 params) with Reconcile(sessionKey): observe {spec,status}, call deriveConvergeActions, execute under existing locks. Per-key scope dissolves the mega-function.

corePatterns.name: Rate-limited workqueue (per-item exp backoff + jitter + global token bucket + dedup + Forget)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Per-item baseDelay·2^failures capped, plus a global 10/s token bucket and key-dedup; jitter spreads retries so simultaneous failures don't thunder.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Kills the synchronized-respawn-storm (152 sessions failing at t=0 no longer all retry at t=30) and the retry-treadmill. Replaces 'restart the controller' band-aids. Backoff is transport, not judgment (S19 calls it 'mechanical delivery backoff').

corePatterns.name: Server-Side Apply field ownership (one manager per field, fully-specified intent)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Each field has exactly one owning manager; you declare the whole set you own; conflicts are explicit errors, not last-write-wins; unmentioned owned fields are deleted.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Single-writer-per-field-group via total typed structs (desiredSessionIdentity, CommitStartedPatch). Writing the WHOLE identity group atomically makes 'path X forgot key Y' (the #3872 class) a compile error, not a silent gap. No managedFields apparatus needed.

corePatterns.name: Finalizers (deletionTimestamp blocks closure until cleanup confirmed)
corePatterns.whatItIs: An object with a finalizer key cannot be fully deleted until a controller runs external cleanup and clears the key; teardown is guaranteed even across controller downtime.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: durable.absent teardown intent dominates all actions and blocks bead closure until the runtime pane is observed-gone (#2073). Also inverts the orphan-close default: prove-dead-before-close, fixing the swallowed-probe-error destructive path.

corePatterns.name: Spec/status subresource split
corePatterns.whatItIs: Desired state (spec, user/config-owned) and observed state (status, controller-owned) get separate endpoints, writers, and version tracking, so user edits and controller writes never collide.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Split the ~29-key metadata bag into SessionSpec (identity, pool slot, prompt-configured, wake_mode) and SessionStatus (start hash, primed markers, live_hash, circuit). Reconcile becomes converge(spec,status); the dual raw/Info representation gets a principled home.

corePatterns.name: Optimistic concurrency (resourceVersion compare-and-swap)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Every write carries the last-seen opaque version; the store rejects the write if the version moved, forcing a re-read-and-retry. Enables lock-free concurrent writers.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: A version/updated_at CAS on Dolt/sqlite status writes makes the 'redundant independent observers converge idempotently' invariant SAFE rather than hope-based; today only marker-last ordering and idempotency keys stand in for it.

corePatterns.name: Informer resync period (periodic full re-enqueue for drift detection)
corePatterns.whatItIs: On a long interval the cache re-enqueues every object whether or not it changed, catching any drift missed by dropped events. This is WHY idempotence is non-negotiable.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Reinterpret the fixed 30s tick as the resync floor (drift-catch), not the primary trigger. Edge hints (event bus, fsnotify) drive responsiveness; resync guarantees eventual convergence even if every hint is lost.

primitivesItSuggests: Reconcile(ctx, key) → (Result{RequeueAfter}, error): a per-session contract wrapping a pure decision core plus a thin idempotent executor, replacing the batch mega-function.

A rate-limited per-key work queue: resync + edge-hint enqueue, key dedup, per-item exponential backoff with jitter, global token bucket, Forget-on-success. Import k8s.io/client-go/util/workqueue or a small typed clone.

Typed field-groups with a single owning writer applied as a WHOLE value ('SSA-lite'): SessionSpec and SessionStatus structs, each mutated only by exactly one derive/commit function taking the full struct, never a metadata map.

A finalizer-flavored teardown intent: a durable 'runtime-teardown-pending' fact that dominates all other actions and blocks bead closure until the runtime is observed-absent, guaranteeing no orphaned panes.
A generation/resourceVersion CAS on status writes, making the redundant-observers reliability invariant provably safe under concurrent writers instead of hope-based.

deriveConvergeActions(spec, status) → []action: a pure, total, clock-free core where totality is a type obligation (exhaustive enum switch, no default-skip) and idempotence is a table-tested law (re-derive after apply = ∅).
A trace/WAL replay corpus promoted to first-class test input: recorded (spec, status) observation streams become the fuzz/regression corpus for the pure core.
antipatterns: Importing K8s literally — CRDs, apimachinery, etcd, a real apiserver. The invariant is map IDEAS to idiomatic Go. A bead-backed single process is not a cluster; borrow the discipline, not the dependency tree.

Building the full managedFields per-field-manager registry. Gas City is one process with N code paths, not N controllers fighting. You need single-writer-per-group + total typed writes, not a field-ownership database.

Putting judgment in the queue: 'if backoff exceeded then give up/restart.' Backoff meters WHEN reconcile re-runs (transport); the DECISION to stop or relaunch stays a durable bead/prompt fact. The rate limiter must remain judgment-free to satisfy 'more useful as models improve.'

Dropping the resync tick because the event bus/fsnotify 'already tells us.' That reverts to edge-triggered — a single missed event becomes permanent drift. Keep periodic full re-enqueue as the level-triggered safety net.

Stamping primed_at BEFORE the nudge (at-most-once: crash loses the prompt forever) OR naive inversion nudge-first (unbounded re-nudge). Must be write-ahead intent marker + explicit confirmation — the exact distributed-systems trap S19 already caught.

force-conflict / last-write-wins on every writer (the SSA force-override abuse). Making every path a force-owner reintroduces the silent-clobber the split was meant to kill. Conflicts between two would-be writers should be loud, not forced.

Big-bang rewrite of the 14,400 LOC. S35 is explicit: copy convergence's idioms into a staged rewrite, do NOT merge session-reconcile into convergence. Every stage must be parity-pinned and shadow-soaked to zero divergence before it flips.

Reconcile that reads live state and takes a destructive action from an UNobserved fact (tmux blip → providerAlive=false → close a live session). Observed-gating (S19 C5) is mandatory: no start/teardown/nudge derives from a fact that wasn't actually probed this tick.

Treating the WAL trace subsystem as the debugging endpoint rather than a test oracle. If reconcile is pure and per-key, the trace stream IS a replay corpus; leaving it as a symptom-of-undebuggability tool wastes the asset.
testabilityContribution: The controller-runtime lens yields a military-grade testability stack that Gas City can enumerate rather than sample. (1) The pure, total, clock-free core deriveConvergeActions(spec,status) makes the entire input space the cross-product of two typed field-groups — exhaustively truth-tableable (S19 §7.1) and fuzzable, with three provable properties: totality (every combination, including unknown-status, returns a defined action list — no skip-forever), idempotence (re-derive after actions land = ∅, asserted per row), and determinism (time/randomness enter as facts, so runs are replayable). (2) The legacy-parity pin proves the new core byte-identical to the old paths until a fix is deliberately activated, giving regression-free migration off the 14,400 LOC. (3) Order-recording fakes (fake sessFront + fake worker.Handle that log operation order and inject failure at each step) prove the finalizer/write-ahead ordering and the ≤1-duplicate crash-window bound — the distributed-systems-classic coverage. (4) Shadow mode runs the new core in production against real cities, diffs against legacy, and soaks divergence to zero before any flip. (5) The queue is independently testable with a deterministic fake clock: assert the backoff schedule, dedup, and jitter spread without spinning a single real session. (6) Because reconcile is per-key and pure, the existing WAL-grade trace stream becomes a replay corpus — the debugging tooling that exists BECAUSE the code is undebuggable is repurposed into the test oracle. Net effect: whole error classes (partial-stamp, orphan-teardown, thundering-herd, spec/status-clobber, unknown-state-skip) become table entries you assert are unreachable, rather than incidents you triage after the fact.

title: Military-Grade Deterministic Simulation Testing for a Go Bead-Backed Reconciler
summary:
**Military-grade reliability is not "more tests" — it is making the reconciler *deterministically simulable*, then running millions of seeded, fault-injected histories against invariants, with laptop-reproducible replay.** Four lineages converge on one recipe: FoundationDB's Flow (build the DB inside a single-threaded discrete-event simulator for 18 months before the first real packet), TigerBeetle's VOPR (real code, mocked clock/network/disk, 1000× time compression, 24/7 on 1024 cores, any bug replays from `seed + git-SHA`), Antithesis (a deterministic hypervisor that treats correctness properties as *targets to break*), and Anvil/TLA+ (controller correctness is a **liveness** property — "eventually stable reconciliation" — not merely safety).

**What transfers, and what does not.** Gas City is Go, not Zig/C++/Rust, and was not born deterministic. The trap (Polar Signals' "mostly DST": WASM + faketime + a patched runtime) is trying to make the *whole concurrent controller* deterministic. The escape is functional-core / imperative-shell: if the decision logic is a **pure, total `Plan(World) → []Action`** with no clock, no `rand`, no IO, no goroutines, no globals — time and entropy entering only as *inputs* — that core is deterministic *for free*, sidestepping Go's scheduler entirely. Concurrency, tmux, and Dolt stay in a thin shell exercised by the existing subprocess chaos tier, not by DST.

**This core already exists in embryo.** `internal/session/lifecycle_projection.go` (`ProjectLifecycle`, `LifecycleInput` with `Now time.Time` as a field, "does not perform runtime I/O", fully typed `BaseState`/`DesiredState`/`RuntimeProjection` enums, a single `LifecycleInputFromMetadata` codec edge) is exactly the FDB/Anvil "reconcile as a pure state machine" shape. `internal/convergence/reconcile.go` is the sound sibling: typed-state `switch`, `(action,error)` wrapped at one site, idempotency-key lookups, marker-last write ordering, crash-safe startup reconcile, and — critically — `unknown state ⇒ error`, not skip-forever. S19's `observe→diff→act` with a legacy-parity pin is the same idea staged onto the god files.

**The simulated world is 80% built.** `runtime.NewFake()`, an in-memory bead store, and `clock.Fake{Advance()}` already exist; the chaos harness proved INV-1 ("every started unit eventually completes") over four reversible injectors with seed-replay. The missing piece is a *pure-core* DST loop **below** the subprocess tier: a discrete-event `step()` scheduler driving the shell single-threaded, a seeded fault decorator on the store/provider seams (BUGGIFY-style: drop/delay/error/crash-between-writes), a simulated clock (the chaos doc flags `CityRuntime` hardcoding `clock.Real{}` as the gap), and an invariant engine asserting safety every step and liveness after quiescence.

**The migration lever is a differential oracle.** Run the 2,188-line god-function and the new pure `Plan` against the *same seeded world* and assert identical action streams — S19's parity pin generalized. This strangles the god files behind a simulator safety-net the way FDB built in simulation first, and it makes the WAL-grade `gc trace` subsystem redundant: a pure core plus `seed+SHA` reproduces any failure on a laptop, which is precisely what the trace tooling strains to approximate.

corePatterns.name: Deterministic Simulation Testing (DST / VOPR)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Run real code single-threaded in a discrete-event simulator with mocked clock/IO; a seed governs workload and faults; time compresses; any failure replays exactly from seed plus commit.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: A pure-core step() loop below the subprocess tier: the seed picks workload plus fault schedule, sim-clock advances only when quiescent, invariants checked each tick, seed+SHA printed on failure for laptop replay.

corePatterns.name: Functional core, imperative shell
corePatterns.whatItIs: Split logic into a pure, side-effect-free decision function and a thin shell doing all IO. The core needs no mocks and is deterministic by construction.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Complete ProjectLifecycle into a total Plan(World)->[]Action; the shell observes beads+runtime, calls Plan, executes actions under existing locks. Sidesteps Go's scheduler non-determinism for the decision logic.

corePatterns.name: Reconcile-as-pure-state-machine (level-triggered)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Each tick derives actions from full observed state, not the triggering event. Idempotent and self-healing: re-running after actions land yields no further actions.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Recompute from {durableFacts, runtimeFacts} every tick; eliminates edge-triggered per-path metadata-stamping gaps where 100+ bugs live. internal/convergence already does this — promote the idiom into the session reconciler.

corePatterns.name: Simulated world (fakes as faithful models)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Replace real IO with in-memory implementations that faithfully model semantics — not stubs: fake store, fake provider, injectable clock, seeded event queue — the whole system in one process.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Compose existing runtime.NewFake() + memstore + clock.Fake behind a discrete-event scheduler. Close the flagged gap: CityRuntime hardcodes clock.Real{} — inject the Clock so simulated time drives the loop.

corePatterns.name: Fault injection at the seams (BUGGIFY)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Wrap IO boundaries with a seeded decorator that drops, delays, errors, and crashes between writes, biased toward dangerous states — the main engine surfacing concurrency and crash bugs.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: A fault decorator over BeadStore and runtime.Provider: crash between a marker's two writes, error a close, delay an observe. Faults must be reversible or 'eventually completes' hangs instead of failing.

corePatterns.name: Liveness invariants / Eventually-Stable Reconciliation
corePatterns.whatItIs: Controller correctness is a liveness property — eventually reach and stay at desired state despite faults — requiring explicit fairness/quiescence assumptions, not just always-true safety checks.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Assert-always safety (no live session's bead closed; <=one retry per eval) plus assert-after-quiesce liveness (durable converges to runtime within K ticks; all started work terminal). INV-1..14 already catalog these.

corePatterns.name: Property-based + state-machine testing with shrinking
corePatterns.whatItIs: Generate random command sequences against a model, assert invariants, and automatically shrink any failing sequence to a minimal reproducer. pgregory.net/rapid provides this for Go.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: rapid drives random {config edit, bead event, injected fault} sequences through the sim; on violation it shrinks to the minimal history and bridges to go test -fuzz via rapid.MakeFuzz.

corePatterns.name: Differential / metamorphic oracle (parity pin)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Run two implementations (or one under a semantics-preserving transform) on identical inputs and assert equivalent results — the safety net when refactoring critical code.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Run the 2,188-line god-function and new Plan on the same seeded world; assert identical action streams. S19's legacy-parity pin generalized — strangle the god files behind the simulator.

primitivesItSuggests: Plan(World) -> []Action: a single pure, total decision function — the one reconciler primitive. Full observed state in, ordered idempotent actions out. Makes edge-triggered per-path stamping gaps unrepresentable.

A typed World/Snapshot value: {DesiredConfig, DurableFacts, RuntimeFacts, Now, Entropy} replacing 29 raw metadata strings and the dual bead/Info representation. Time and randomness are fields, not ambient calls.

A closed Action sum type plus a single Executor — the only place with side effects. Collapses the triplicated attach pipelines, 5 normalization sites, and 3 scope-close copies into one gated executor where a tmux-blip->close can be interlocked.
Typed lifecycle-state enums with exhaustive switch and unknown => error: extend the existing BaseState / convergence-state pattern so an unhandled state is a typed failure, not a silent skip-forever.

Write-ahead intent markers as a beads-native primitive: an intent bead written BEFORE an at-most-once side effect and confirmed after, so recovery distinguishes 'never attempted' from 'attempted, outcome unknown'. Generalizes S19's priming_attempted; makes the at-most-once/at-least-once delivery bug class unrepresentable.
An injected Clock and Entropy seam (interfaces) replacing hardcoded clock.Real{} and global rand — the enabling seam for a simulated world.

A simworld test package: fake store + fake provider + sim clock + seeded fault decorator + discrete-event scheduler + invariant engine — Gas City's reusable VOPR, sitting below the subprocess chaos tier.
A differential-oracle harness that runs the legacy god-function and the new core on one seeded world asserting equivalence — the migration safety net.
antipatterns: Trying to make the whole concurrent controller (goroutines + real tmux + real Dolt) deterministic instead of factoring out a pure core — you fight Go's scheduler (WASM/faketime pain) for little gain. The subprocess chaos tier is valuable soak/integration but is NOT DST (it hardcodes clock.Real{}); don't conflate the two.

Mocks that return canned values instead of faithful in-memory models. A stub tests nothing — the fake store/provider must be a real model of the semantics (like memstore), or the simulator proves the mock rather than the system.

Asserting on events, log lines, counts, or timing instead of durable bead state. Events are best-effort and controller-resident; the store is ground truth. Timing/count assertions are flaky and reintroduce non-determinism.

Asserting liveness during faults, or against a constant deadline. Without a quiescence/fairness model you get false positives; the deadline must be read from live state (e.g. quarantined_until) and liveness checked only after faults clear.
Non-reversible fault injection. A fault that irrecoverably corrupts the store makes 'eventually completes' HANG rather than fail — an invisible false-green. Every injected fault must be recoverable.

Sprinkling seeds/determinism onto a god function after the fact. DST works because the core is born pure (TigerBeetle/FDB were designed for it). A 28-parameter function with hidden time.Now()/rand/map-iteration/goroutines can never be made replayable.

Hidden nondeterminism leaking into the 'pure' core: time.Now(), global math/rand, map-iteration order in action ordering, wall-clock in idempotency keys, background goroutines. One leak silently breaks replay — needs a vet/lint gate forbidding those in the core package.

Running the simulator once (an overnight sweep) instead of continuously. DST value compounds with CPU-hours (TigerBeetle: 24/7 on 1024 cores; FDB: ~1 trillion CPU-hours). A one-shot run finds only shallow bugs.

Too-gentle fault distributions that never drive the system into interesting states. FDB tunes distributions and uses BUGGIFY to bias toward danger; an injector that never crashes mid-write only ever proves the happy path.
testabilityContribution: This paradigm supplies the entire military-grade spine: (1) a pure Plan core that is exhaustively unit-testable without mocks and deterministic by construction; (2) a simulated world running the REAL decision code against a fake store/provider/clock at compressed time; (3) a seeded VOPR loop exploring millions of fault-injected histories and printing seed+SHA for exact laptop replay — retiring the WAL-grade gc trace subsystem that exists only because today's code is otherwise irreproducible; (4) property-based / state-machine tests (rapid) that auto-shrink any violating history to a minimal reproducer and bridge to Go's coverage-guided fuzzer; (5) safety invariants asserted every tick and liveness/ESR invariants asserted after quiescence (INV-1..14 already enumerated); (6) a differential oracle that de-risks the god-file migration by proving byte-identical behavior before cutover. Every future bug becomes a permanent regression seed. Net effect is defense-in-depth: pure-core unit tests -> property/state-machine tests -> in-memory DST -> subprocess chaos soak -> production assertions — the same layered posture TigerBeetle and FoundationDB credit for their reliability.

title: Reconciler + State-Convergence Theory for a Bead-Backed Go Controller
summary:
The reconciler's essential job, stripped of accidental complexity, is a **level-triggered control loop over a durable Snapshot**. Kubernetes' core lesson: don't react to events, react to *current state*. Watch events (`bead.updated`, fsnotify, the 30s tick) are merely *nudges to re-derive*, never instructions. That single choice is why the loop survives missed edges, crashes, and partitions — precisely the property Gas City's #3872 bug class proves is missing today, where each arrival path (spawn/adopt/resume/drift/rollback) stamps its own subset of side effects edge-triggered.

The cleanest formal shape is a three-function pipeline, already latent in the repo's landed S19 stage-1 core and the `internal/convergence` blueprint:

```
Observe : World    -> Snapshot     // impure, fail-closed
Decide  : Snapshot -> []Effect     // pure, total, empty at fixpoint
Apply   : Effect   -> World        // impure, idempotent per effect
```

This is Functional Core / Imperative Shell (Bernhardt) fused with Chassaing's Decider (`decide`/`evolve` as pure functions) and Redux's `(state,action)→state`, with **effects-as-data**: Decide returns a closed sum type of Effects; a thin interpreter Applies them. Snapshot = `{durableFacts` from beads, `runtimeFacts` from a live probe`}` built once per tick — the landed `durableFacts`/`runtimeFacts`/`deriveConvergeActions` already are exactly this.

Why it self-heals: **level-triggered + idempotent = convergent**. Idempotency is non-negotiable because Decide runs many times over the same state; re-running after effects land must yield `[]` (the repo's `TestDeriveConvergeActionsIdempotent`). CRDT theory sharpens the intuition: a convergent merge is commutative, associative, and idempotent, each mapping to a real failure mode — idempotency defeats duplication, commutativity defeats reorder, associativity defeats batching. Effects must be *absolute* ("set primed_at"), never *relative* ("append"), so replay is safe.

Beads are the durable Snapshot substrate: one metadata key = one fact (single source of truth, retiring the ~350-LOC precedence ladders and their oscillation bugs). Idempotency keys (`FindByIdempotencyKey`) are the dedup layer. The write-ahead intent trap is the crown jewel: priming is *at-most-once* if you stamp `primed_at` before the nudge (a crash loses the prompt forever) and *unbounded at-least-once* if you invert — solved by a WAL pair living in beads: `priming_attempted_at` (write-ahead) + `primed_at` (confirmation), which is ARIES redo-logging in miniature.

**Illegal states unrepresentable**: replace the stringly-typed state (unknown ⇒ skip-forever, no TTL) with a sum-typed Effect enum and total switch (the `exhaustive` linter approximates the compiler check Go lacks). A total Decide over sum types makes "unhandled state" a build error, not a silent every-tick re-log.

"Keep judgment out of Go" fits a pure Decide precisely *because* Decide routes effects, never reasons: "durable=absent ∧ runtime=live ⇒ rollback" is mechanical bookkeeping, not `if stuck then restart`. A total, inspectable Decide is the *most honest* enforcement point for judgment-out-of-Go — every branch is visible and table-tested, so a reviewer can prove none encodes model-substitutable reasoning. As models improve, the *effects* (nudge, prompt) grow more capable while Decide stays fixed.

Relation to the S-series: this paradigm *is* the destination S19 is walking toward incrementally. Stage 1 landed the pure cores dormant behind a legacy-parity pin. Migration path: keep the pin, port each edge-triggered side-effect site into an Effect + Decide branch, prove byte-parity, flip the pin per-fix. S35's negative result stands — do NOT merge the session and convergence reconcilers; they share the *pattern*, not domain/state, so each gets its own `Decide` instantiated from one shared generic seam.

corePatterns.name: Level-triggered reconciliation (events are nudges, not instructions)
corePatterns.whatItIs: React to current observed state, not to individual events; re-derive desired state each pass so missed/duplicated/reordered edges self-correct. The trigger is edge-driven; the behavior is level-based.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: The 30s tick + bead.updated + fsnotify only nudge the loop to re-Observe. Every session's actions are re-derived each tick from durable beads, so a dropped edge (#3872) heals next pass instead of stranding the session forever.

corePatterns.name: Observe -> Decide -> Apply (Functional Core / Imperative Shell)
corePatterns.whatItIs: A pure, total Decide sits between an impure Observe (build a Snapshot) and an impure Apply (perform effects). All I/O is confined to the shell; the core is a value-to-value function.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Observe builds durableFacts (bead metadata) + runtimeFacts (tmux/provider probe) once per tick; deriveConvergeActions decides; the reconciler Applies under existing alias locks and the worker boundary. Confines side effects to Layer 0.

corePatterns.name: Decider pattern: pure decide + evolve over total functions
corePatterns.whatItIs: Chassaing's decide: (Cmd,State)->[]Event and evolve: (State,Event)->State, both pure with an explicit initialState and isTerminal. Business logic becomes store-agnostic, mock-free, replayable data.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Decide=deriveConvergeActions(durable,runtime)->[]Effect; evolve = re-Observe after Apply. isTerminal maps to the convergence bead's terminated state. Layer idempotency by threading an idempotency-key set exactly as Equinox describes.

corePatterns.name: Idempotency + convergence => self-healing (fixpoint law)
corePatterns.whatItIs: If Decide is idempotent and the loop level-triggered, the system is a stable goal-seeker: bugs self-correct, restarts need no event replay, and no-op reconciliations are the common case.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Mandate Decide(Snapshot) == [] once effects have landed; TestDeriveConvergeActionsIdempotent enforces it. Redundant observers checking the same durable beads converge — the repo's stated reliability mechanism (redundancy), now provable not review-asserted.

corePatterns.name: Effects-as-data + interpreter (Elm/Redux-middleware)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Decide returns a closed set of Effect values describing WHAT should happen; a separate runtime interprets/executes them. Description of effects is decoupled from their execution.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: sessConvergeAction enum (rollback, stampIdentity, stampPrimed, attemptPrime) is the effect language; the reconciler is the interpreter. New arrival paths add zero new effect sites — they just feed facts into the one Decide.

corePatterns.name: Write-ahead intent log (at-most-once vs at-least-once delivery)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Stamp a durable intent marker BEFORE a side effect, confirm AFTER. A crash between them replays at-least-once; idempotency + confirmation collapses that to effectively-once. Absolute (not relative) effects make replay safe.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: priming_attempted_at (write-ahead) + primed_at (confirmation) living in bead metadata make startup-prompt delivery crash-safe: a crash re-delivers at most one duplicate per backoff interval instead of losing the prompt forever or nudging every tick.

corePatterns.name: Make illegal states unrepresentable (sum types, total switch)
corePatterns.whatItIs: Model 'exactly one of these kinds' as a tagged union so impossible states can't be constructed, and write total functions with exhaustive switches the compiler/linter forces you to complete.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Replace the raw session-state string (unknown => skip-forever, re-logged each tick) with a typed state/Effect sum + exhaustive linter. Kills the ~29 stringly-typed keys / 252 access sites and the default:skip-forever black hole.

corePatterns.name: Single source of truth: beads as durable Snapshot
corePatterns.whatItIs: One canonical durable location per fact; everything else derives from it. No copied/duplicated state, no per-path precedence ladders reconciling N competing sources by hand.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: One bead metadata key = one durableFact. Stamp a canonical-identity record once (actionStampCanonicalIdentity) instead of re-inferring identity every tick from six sources through 350 LOC of precedence — the documented oscillation-bug generator.

corePatterns.name: CRDT/semilattice convergence intuition
corePatterns.whatItIs: A merge that is commutative, associative, and idempotent converges regardless of delivery order, duplication, or batching (strong eventual consistency, CALM/monotonicity). Each law defends against one network failure mode.
corePatterns.gasCityApplication: Design each Effect so applying it is monotone/absolute and order-insensitive; then multiple independent observers Applying overlapping action lists converge without a coordinator — matching the SDK-self-sufficiency + redundancy invariants.

primitivesItSuggests: A generic reconciler seam Reconciler[Snapshot, Effect] with three injected functions: Observe(World)->Snapshot, Decide(Snapshot)->[]Effect (pure/total), Apply(Effect)->error. Both session and convergence reconcilers instantiate it without sharing domain state (honors S35).

Facts value objects: durableFacts (from beads, one field per metadata key) + runtimeFacts (from a single live probe), built once per tick. Time and probe results enter as fields so the core is clock-free and I/O-free.
A closed Effect sum type per reconciler (Go: named int enum + exhaustive linter, or interface-union) so Decide is a total function and 'unhandled state' is a lint/build error.

A tri-state observation type (Unknown/Present/Absent) plus an `observed` gate so an unprobed or fail-closed runtime can never authorize a destructive effect — encodes the tmux-blip safety invariant in the type.

An IntentLog primitive: a durable (attempted_at, confirmed_at, subject_hash) marker triple on a bead that turns any at-least-once delivery into effectively-once with bounded retry (generalizes priming to nudge/spawn/teardown).
Idempotency keys as a first-class bead capability (FindByIdempotencyKey already exists) used uniformly for wisp pour, session create, and effect dedup.
A convergence law harness: property tests asserting Decide is total (never panics on random facts) and idempotent at fixpoint (Decide after Apply == nil), reused across every reconciler instance.

Snapshot-as-data replay: because Snapshot is a serializable value, a captured production Snapshot re-runs Decide offline — the gc trace WAL becomes a first-class Decide input, not a forensic-only artifact.

A legacy-parity pin harness: a shared switch that runs new Decide alongside the legacy path and asserts byte-identical effects until a fix is deliberately activated (the S19 migration mechanism, generalized).
antipatterns: Bolting a level-triggered outer loop on top while keeping edge-triggered side-effect stamping INSIDE it — the current reconciler's disease. The loop shape is necessary but not sufficient; the decision itself must be re-derived from state, not path.

Making Decide impure 'just this once' — reading the store, wall clock, or firing I/O inside the decision. Destroys totality, idempotency, and table-testability. Time must enter as a fact (now, primingAttemptedAt), never be read by the core.

Proving idempotence/equivalence by review comment ('equivalence-proven') instead of by construction. The 18 raw/Info predicate pairs each carry a proof-by-review; a single pure Decide makes the property mechanically testable instead.
Stringly-typed state with default: skip-forever — unknown states silently stall with no TTL, no escalation, re-logged every tick. Sum types + exhaustive switch turn the same gap into a build failure.

Stamping the confirmation before the side effect (at-most-once: crash loses the effect) OR re-firing every tick with no marker (unbounded at-least-once). Both are the priming trap; only the WAL pair (attempt-then-confirm) is correct.

Swallowing Observe errors so a transient probe blip (tmux hiccup) reads as providerAlive=false and authorizes a destructive orphan-close of a live session. Observe must fail-closed; unobserved runtime may authorize NO teardown (the observed-gating flag).
Treating events as instructions to replay in order rather than as nudges to re-derive. Forces you to never miss/reorder an edge — the fragility level-triggering exists to eliminate.
Emitting non-idempotent, relative effects ('append child', 'increment') instead of absolute assignments ('set block X to Y', 'set primed_at'). Relative effects corrupt state on replay.

Merging distinct reconcilers because they 'share a pattern.' S35's landed negative result: session and convergence reconcilers share the observe->diff->act PATTERN, not domain/state/code — copy the idioms, don't unify the code.

One 2,188-line / 28-parameter god function as the 'reconciler.' The trace WAL subsystem and gc trace CLI exist because that code is otherwise undebuggable — the tooling is a symptom of the missing pure core, not a substitute for it.
Re-inferring identity every tick from six competing sources via precedence ladders — nondeterministic, oscillation-prone. One healed canonical fact, written once, read by all.
testabilityContribution: A pure, total Decide is the single biggest lever for military-grade testability: (1) Exhaustive TABLE tests over the (durableFacts × runtimeFacts) cross-product with zero tmux/provider/Dolt boot and zero mocks — the S19 core already table-tests identity-heal, priming, firstStart, and rollback this way. (2) Two mechanically-checkable LAWS replace proof-by-review: totality (Decide never panics / has no default black hole — enforceable by fuzzing random facts) and idempotence-at-fixpoint (Decide after its effects land returns nil — TestDeriveConvergeActionsIdempotent). (3) Sum-typed Effects + exhaustive switch (the `exhaustive` linter, since Go lacks native unions) make an unhandled state a build failure, not a silent runtime stall. (4) CRDT laws give property tests for reorder/dup/batch robustness: shuffle and duplicate action lists, assert the same converged World. (5) Snapshot-as-data means a captured production Snapshot re-runs Decide offline deterministically, turning the trace WAL from a forensic afterthought into a regression fixture — every incident becomes a permanent table-test row. (6) The imperative shell shrinks to a thin, conditional-free interpreter that is the only thing needing integration/e2e coverage, so the hard-to-test surface is minimized and the subtle logic is 100% unit-testable.
